install.-m 755 app /usr/local/bin/app: Copy app to /usr/local/bin/app and set executable mode 755
install.-d -m 755 /opt/app: Create /opt/app and missing parent directories with mode 755
install.-o root -g root file /etc/app/file: Copy file and set owner root and group root
install.-C -m 644 config.yml /etc/app/config.yml: Copy only when content, owner or permissions would change
install.-D -m 755 app /usr/local/bin/app: Create leading destination directories, then install app
install.-b -S .bak file /etc/app/file: Back up the existing destination with .bak suffix before copying
install.--backup: Make a backup of each existing destination file
install.--backup=: Choose backup control mode such as numbered, existing or simple
install.-b: Make a backup using the default backup control
install.-C: Compare source and destination and skip unchanged files
install.--compare: Compare source and destination and skip unchanged files
install.-d: Treat arguments as directory names and create them
install.--directory: Treat arguments as directory names and create them
install.-D: Create leading destination directories, then copy the source
install.--debug: Explain how each file is copied
install.-g: Set group ownership on installed files
install.--group: Set group ownership on installed files
install.-m: Set permission mode instead of the default 755
install.--mode: Set permission mode instead of the default 755
install.-o: Set file owner, usually requiring superuser rights
install.--owner: Set file owner, usually requiring superuser rights
install.-p: Preserve source access and modification timestamps
install.--preserve-timestamps: Preserve source access and modification timestamps
install.-s: Strip symbol tables from installed binaries
install.--strip: Strip symbol tables from installed binaries
install.--strip-program: Select the program used for stripping binaries
install.-t: Copy all source arguments into the target directory
install.--target-directory: Copy all source arguments into the target directory
install.-T: Treat destination as a normal file, not a directory
install.--no-target-directory: Treat destination as a normal file, not a directory
install.-v: Print the name of each created file or directory
install.--verbose: Print the name of each created file or directory
install.-Z: Set the destination SELinux security context to the default type
install.--context: Set the SELinux or SMACK security context

nohup.npm start > app.log 2>&1 &: npm start with log file from the terminal
nohup.python app.py > app.log 2>&1 &: Let the Python app continue to run with the log file
nohup.node server.js > server.log 2>&1 &: Let the node server continue to run with the log file
nohup../worker.sh > worker.log 2>&1 &: Let the worker script continue to run with the log file

nohup.sleep 3600 &: Protect sleep process against terminal end
nohup.java -jar app.jar > app.log 2>&1 &: Let the Java app continue to run with the log file
nohup../backup.sh >> backup.log 2>&1 &: Attach backup script to log file
nohup.tail -f /var/log/syslog > syslog.follow: Keep log followers running in the background
nohup.command &: Protect command against HUP and start in background
nohup../server.sh &: Allow the command to continue running after logout
nohup.long-command > output.log 2>&1 &: Redirect output and let command continue running

jobs.-p %1: only show process ID of job 1
jobs.-l %%: Show current job with process ID
jobs.-p %%: only show process ID of current job

fg.%%: bring current job to the foreground

fg.%nano: Bring job with name nano to the foreground

bg.%%: continue current job in the background

bg.%?python: Continue the job in the background with python in the command
bg.%vim: Continue job with name vim in the background
bg.%nano: Continue the job with the name nano in the background

numfmt.--padding: Pad formatted output to a width

shuf.-n: Return only this many random lines
shuf.--head-count: Return only this many random lines
shuf.-o: Write shuffled output to a file
shuf.--output: Write shuffled output to a file
shuf.--random-source: Read random bytes from a file

truncate.-r: Use another file size as reference
truncate.--reference: Use another file size as reference

od.--endian: Select byte order for multi-byte data

strings.-t: Print offsets in the selected radix
strings.--radix: Print offsets in the selected radix
strings.-f: Print the file name before each string
strings.--print-file-name: Print the file name before each string

sum.-s: Use the System V checksum algorithm
sum.--sysv: Use the System V checksum algorithm

logger.-s: Also write the message to stderr
logger.--stderr: Also write the message to stderr
logger.-f: Read log message from a file
logger.--file: Read log message from a file
logger.-n: Send to a remote syslog server
logger.--server: Send to a remote syslog server

cal.-3: Show previous, current, and next month

sync.-f: Flush the file system containing the path
sync.--file-system: Flush the file system containing the path

test.-f: Check that a path is a regular file
test.-d: Check that a path is a directory
test.-r: Check that a file is readable
test.-w: Check that a file is writable
test.-x: Check that a file is executable
test.-n: Check that a string is not empty
test.-z: Check that a string is empty
test.<left> -gt <right>: Check whether the left integer is greater

logs.--tail: Limit the number of last log lines
Limit the number of last log lines
Limit the number of last log lines
logs.--previous: View logs from previous container run

apply.--dry-run=server: Validate change on the server side

upgrade.--atomic: Automatically roll back in the event of an error
